title: Katherinea zebrina
intro: Katherinea zebrina, also known as Zebrina or Katherine's Zebrina, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Traditionally, Katherinea zebrina has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Katherinea zebrina include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Katherinea zebrina is often prepared in the form of a tincture, infusion, or decoction, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, colds, and skin conditions.

The plant's classification places it within the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that also includes mint, basil, and honeysuckle. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, including its leaf structure, flower arrangement, and fruit characteristics. Katherinea zebrina is a member of the tribe Ruinae, a group of plants characterized by their showy flowers and distinctive leaf shapes.
